examples.title: Basic Dynamic Memory Allocation with malloc
examples.description: Demonstrates allocating memory for a single integer using malloc, writing to it via pointer, and freeing the memory
examples.explanation:
1. malloc(sizeof(int)) requests bytes equal to size of integer from heap
2. Returns NULL if insufficient memory available
3. (int *) casts the generic void* pointer to int*
4. *ptr dereferences to store value at that memory location
5. free() releases memory back to system
6. Setting ptr = NULL prevents dangling pointer dereference

examples.title: Dynamic Array with malloc, realloc, and free
examples.description: Demonstrates creating a resizable dynamic array that grows as needed using realloc
examples.explanation:
1. malloc creates initial array of 3 integers
2. realloc dynamically resizes array when full - preserves existing data
3. If realloc fails, original pointer remains valid - we check temp first
4. arr[i] uses pointer arithmetic: *(arr + i)
5. This demonstrates dynamic array pattern used in vector implementations

examples.title: Linked List with Complete Dynamic Memory Management
examples.description: Demonstrates creating, traversing, and properly freeing a singly linked list with dynamic memory at each node
examples.explanation:
1. Each node allocated individually on heap using malloc
2. createNode returns pointer to newly allocated node
3. insertAtEnd takes **head (pointer to pointer) to modify caller's pointer
4. Each malloc MUST have corresponding free to prevent memory leaks
5. freeList iterates through list, freeing each node one by one
6. After freeList, set head = NULL to avoid dangling reference
7. This pattern is essential for proper resource management in C
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for insertion and display, O(n) for freeing
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of nodes
